warriors.subject: Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj
warriors.text: Shivaji was a great Maratha warrior who founded the Maratha Empire. He fought against the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb to protect his land and people. Shivaji used guerrilla warfare tactics to defeat larger Mughal armies. His mother's name was Jija Bai, who inspired him deeply. Shivaji was crowned as Chhatrapati in 1674 at Raigad.

questions.question: Who did Shivaji fight against?
What is Shivaji's mother's name?
What type of warfare did Shivaji use?
Where was Shivaji crowned as Chhatrapati?

warriors.subject: Maharana Pratap
warriors.text: Maharana Pratap was the Rajput king of Mewar. He refused to accept Mughal Emperor Akbar's supremacy. He fought the famous Battle of Haldighati in 1576 against Man Singh of Amber, who served Akbar. His loyal horse Chetak saved his life in that battle. Pratap died in 1597, but never surrendered to the Mughals.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Maharana Pratap fight against?
Which battle is Maharana Pratap famous for?
What was the name of Maharana Pratap's loyal horse?
Did Maharana Pratap ever surrender to the Mughals?

warriors.subject: Prithviraj Chauhan
warriors.text: Prithviraj Chauhan was a powerful Rajput king of Ajmer and Delhi. He fought against Muhammad Ghori, not the Mughals, as the Mughals came later. He defeated Ghori in the First Battle of Tarain (1191) but lost the Second Battle of Tarain (1192). He is remembered for his bravery, patriotism, and love for Sanyogita. His story is told in the epic poem Prithviraj Raso.

questions.question: Who was Prithviraj Chauhan's main enemy?
Which battle did Prithviraj win?
In which year did Prithviraj lose the final battle?
What is the famous poem about Prithviraj Chauhan?

warriors.subject: Rani Chennamma (Kittur)
warriors.text: Rani Chennamma was the queen of Kittur in Karnataka. She fought against the British, not directly the Mughals. However, her kingdom had earlier resisted Mughal influence. She led an armed rebellion against the British East India Company in 1824. She was captured and died in prison. She is one of India's first female freedom fighters.

questions.question: Who did Rani Chennamma primarily fight against?
Which kingdom did Rani Chennamma rule?
In which year did she lead the rebellion?
What happened to Rani Chennamma at the end?

warriors.subject: Rani Lakshmi Bai (Jhansi)
warriors.text: Rani Lakshmi Bai was the queen of Jhansi. She fought against the British in the 1857 Rebellion, not directly the Mughals, as Mughals had weakened by then. However, she allied with Mughal prince Bahadur Shah Zafar. She died fighting on the battlefield in Gwalior in 1858 dressed as a man. She became a symbol of Indian resistance.

questions.question: Who did Rani Lakshmi Bai fight against?
In which rebellion did she fight?
Where did Rani Lakshmi Bai die?
Which Mughal prince did she ally with?

warriors.subject: Sambhaji Maharaj
warriors.text: Sambhaji Maharaj was the son of Shivaji and the second Maratha Chhatrapati. He fought against the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb for nine years. He was captured by Aurangzeb's forces through betrayal. Aurangzeb tortured him brutally for weeks and executed him in 1689. Sambhaji never converted to Islam nor surrendered.

questions.question: Who was Sambhaji Maharaj's father?
Which Mughal emperor executed Sambhaji?
In which year was Sambhaji executed?
How did Sambhaji die?

warriors.subject: Prahlad Singh (Bundela)
warriors.text: Prahlad Singh was a Bundela Rajput chieftain who fought against the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb. He was an ally of Chhatrasal. He defended his fort against Mughal forces. He was killed in battle. His bravery is remembered in Bundeli folk songs.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Prahlad Singh fight?
Which clan did Prahlad Singh belong to?
Who was Prahlad Singh's famous ally?
How is Prahlad Singh remembered?

warriors.subject: Rani Durgavati (Gondwana)
warriors.text: Rani Durgavati was the ruling queen of Gondwana. She fought against the Mughal forces of Akbar led by Asaf Khan. In 1564, she led her army despite being a widow. She died fighting bravely rather than surrendering. She is remembered as a great warrior queen.

questions.question: Who was Rani Durgavati fighting against?
In which year did she die fighting?
Who led the Mughal army against Rani Durgavati?
What did Rani Durgavati choose over surrender?

warriors.subject: Lachit Borphukan (Ahom Kingdom)
warriors.text: Lachit Borphukan was a general of the Ahom Kingdom in Assam. He defeated the mighty Mughal army led by Ram Singh in the Battle of Saraighat in 1671. He used clever naval tactics on the Brahmaputra river. He died soon after the victory. His name is a symbol of Assamese pride.

questions.question: Which battle made Lachit Borphukan famous?
Which river was key to Lachit's victory?
Who led the Mughal army against Lachit?
In which year was the Battle of Saraighat fought?

warriors.subject: Raja Jaswant Singh (Marwar)
warriors.text: Raja Jaswant Singh was a Rathore ruler of Marwar. He served Shah Jahan and Aurangzeb initially but later fought against Aurangzeb. After his death, Aurangzeb tried to annex Marwar, leading to a long struggle. His son Ajit Singh continued the fight. Jaswant Singh is remembered as a brave Rajput leader.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Jaswant Singh later fight against?
What was Jaswant Singh's kingdom?
Who continued the fight after Jaswant Singh's death?
What did Aurangzeb try to do after Jaswant Singh's death?

warriors.subject: Durgadas Rathore
warriors.text: Durgadas Rathore was a Rajput warrior who fought Aurangzeb for 30 years. He protected the young prince Ajit Singh of Marwar from Mughals. He led a guerrilla war against the Mughal Empire. He never accepted Mughal authority. He is called the 'Savior of Marwar'.

questions.question: Who did Durgadas Rathore fight against?
How many years did Durgadas fight the Mughals?
Whom did Durgadas protect?
What is Durgadas Rathore called?

warriors.subject: Chhatrasal Bundela
warriors.text: Chhatrasal was a Bundela Rajput leader who fought against the Mughals. He was inspired by Shivaji. He established his own kingdom in Bundelkhand. He fought Aurangzeb and later the Marathas helped him. He is a folk hero in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Chhatrasal fight?
Who inspired Chhatrasal?
Which region did Chhatrasal rule?
Who helped Chhatrasal later?

warriors.subject: Guru Gobind Singh
warriors.text: Guru Gobind Singh was the tenth Sikh Guru. He fought against the Mughals and their allied hill kings. He created the Khalsa in 1699. His four sons were martyred by the Mughals. He fought several battles including the Battle of Chamkaur and Muktsar.

questions.question: Which empire did Guru Gobind Singh fight against?
What did Guru Gobind Singh create in 1699?
How many of his sons were martyred?
Name one battle fought by Guru Gobind Singh.

warriors.subject: Banda Singh Bahadur
warriors.text: Banda Singh Bahadur was a Sikh warrior who led an uprising against the Mughals after Guru Gobind Singh's death. He attacked and defeated Mughal forces in Sirhind and punished the killers of Guru's sons. He was captured and executed by the Mughals in 1716.

questions.question: Who inspired Banda Singh Bahadur?
Which city did Banda Singh Bahadur capture from Mughals?
What happened to Banda Singh Bahadur at the end?
In which year was he executed?

warriors.subject: Hemu (Hemu Vikramaditya)
warriors.text: Hemu was a Hindu king who fought against the Mughals under Akbar. He won 22 battles across North India. In 1556, he captured Delhi and declared himself king. He fought the Second Battle of Panipat against Akbar's forces led by Bairam Khan. He was wounded and captured.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Hemu fight?
How many battles did Hemu win?
Which battle did Hemu lose?
Who led Akbar's army against Hemu?

warriors.subject: Rana Sanga (Mewar)
warriors.text: Rana Sanga was the Rajput ruler of Mewar. He fought against Babar, the first Mughal emperor, at the Battle of Khanwa in 1527. He was wounded many times in battle. After his defeat, the Mughal rule became stronger in India. He died in 1528.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Rana Sanga fight?
Name the battle of Rana Sanga against Babar.
In which year was the Battle of Khanwa?
What happened to Rana Sanga after the battle?

warriors.subject: Medini Rai (Malwa)
warriors.text: Medini Rai was a powerful Rajput leader in Malwa. He fought against Babar at the Battle of Chanderi in 1528. Babar defeated him and captured the fort. Medini Rai and his followers performed Jauhar (self-immolation) instead of surrendering.

questions.question: Who defeated Medini Rai?
Which fort's battle is Medini Rai known for?
What is Jauhar?
In which year was the Battle of Chanderi?

warriors.subject: Dulla Bhatti (Punjab)
warriors.text: Dulla Bhatti was a Punjabi folk hero who rebelled against the Mughal emperor Akbar. He is famous for helping poor girls get married. He was captured and executed in 1599. His name lives on in Punjabi folk songs.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Dulla Bhatti fight?
What is Dulla Bhatti famous for?
What happened to Dulla Bhatti in 1599?
In which region's folk songs is Dulla Bhatti remembered?

warriors.subject: Tilka Manjhi (Santhal)
warriors.text: Tilka Manjhi was a Santhal tribal leader who fought against the Mughals and later the British. He led an uprising against Mughal tax collectors in 1771. He was captured and killed by the British in 1785. He is considered the first Adivasi freedom fighter.

questions.question: Who did Tilka Manjhi first fight against?
Which tribe did Tilka Manjhi belong to?
In which year did he lead an uprising against Mughal tax?
Who killed Tilka Manjhi?

warriors.subject: Rao Tula Ram (Rewari)
warriors.text: Rao Tula Ram was a Jat king of Rewari. He fought against the Mughals during the 1857 rebellion. His ancestors had fought Aurangzeb earlier. He captured Rewari from the Mughals. He went to Persia for help but died there.

questions.question: Which rebellion did Rao Tula Ram fight in?
Which Mughal emperor did his ancestors fight?
From where did Rao Tula Ram capture control?
Where did Rao Tula Ram go for help?

warriors.subject: Bheema Nayak (Karnataka)
warriors.text: Bheema Nayak was a chieftain from Karnataka who fought against the Mughals under Aurangzeb. He led a rebellion in the 1680s. He used guerrilla tactics in hilly areas. He was captured and executed. He is a folk hero in North Karnataka.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Bheema Nayak fight?
Which state is Bheema Nayak from?
What tactics did Bheema Nayak use?
How did Bheema Nayak die?

warriors.subject: Mankoji Dahatonde (Maratha)
warriors.text: Mankoji Dahatonde was a Maratha warrior who fought against the Mughals before Shivaji rose to power. He served Shahaji, Shivaji's father. He fought many battles in the Deccan against Mughal commanders. He died fighting. His son continued the struggle.

questions.question: Which empire did Mankoji fight?
Whom did Mankoji serve?
Where did Mankoji fight?
What happened to Mankoji?

warriors.subject: Sangram Shah (Garha Katanga)
warriors.text: Sangram Shah was a Gond king of Garha Katanga. He fought against the Mughal advance in central India. He resisted Sher Shah Suri too. His kingdom was later attacked by Akbar's forces. He died in battle. His daughter Rani Durgavati continued the fight.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor's forces attacked Sangram Shah's kingdom?
What was Sangram Shah's kingdom?
Who continued the fight after Sangram Shah?
How did Sangram Shah die?

warriors.subject: Murari Rao Ghorpade
warriors.text: Murari Rao was a Maratha general who fought against the Mughals in the Deccan. He served the Maratha Empire under the Peshwas. He captured several Mughal forts. Later he fought against the Nizam who was a Mughal vassal. He is known for his military skills.

questions.question: Which empire did Murari Rao fight against?
Who was Murari Rao's overlord?
What did Murari Rao capture from Mughals?
Who was the Mughal vassal he fought?
